channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Ivory Coast vs Norway: FIFA World Cup Round of 32: Prediction, how to watch
item.description: Erling Haaland's Norway face Yan Diomande-powered Ivory Coast, as the pair clash for a place in the round of 16.

item.title: Millions in India’s Bengal risk losing welfare benefits after vote deletion
item.description: Controversial revision of electoral rolls removed millions. Now, the BJP says they are not eligible for government aid.

item.title: How each prime minister broke Britain’s economy
item.description: How each prime minister broke Britain’s economy

item.title: At the World Cup, the media has set up a moral checkpoint
item.description: While US and European players just get to play football, Global South footballers are questioned about politics.

item.title: DR Congo says 1,307 Ebola cases confirmed, including 377 deaths
item.description: Outbreak spreads to a fourth province, Haut-Uele, bordering South Sudan and the CAR, according to a media report.

item.title: Social media calculator: How much of your life is spent on social media?
item.description: The average user spends roughly two hours, 39 minutes on social media daily, adding up to more than 40 days a year.

item.title: WhatsApp to let users go by usernames, not phone numbers
item.description: WhatsApp says the feature is designed to give its three billion users a new layer of control over who can contact them.

item.title: Peru elects Keiko Fujimori in victory for Latin American right
item.description: Peru's conservative president-elect Keiko Fujimori vows that the ‘doors to dialogue will always be open.’

item.title: What is Iran looking for in any upcoming talks with the US?
item.description: Strait of Hormuz, frozen funds, Lebanon at forefront of the minds of Iran's negotiators ahead of potential talks.

item.title: In pictures: Venezuela earthquakes death toll surpasses 1,700
item.description: Rescuers race to find survivors in Venezuela's quake-hit areas as families cling to hope amid widespread devastation.

item.title: Moment drone hits vehicle in Zaporizhzhia
item.description: Video shows the moment a vehicle was damaged by a drone in Zaporizhzhia, southeastern Ukraine.

item.title: Paraguay declares holiday after beating Germany in World Cup upset
item.description: Paraguay has declared a national holiday after its national team stunned four-time champions Germany

item.title: Chinese billionaire Guo Wengui gets 30 years in US prison for huge fraud
item.description: A federal judge said Guo's fraud cost more than 1,000 people worldwide hundreds of millions of dollars.

item.title: Heartbroken Iran exit World Cup amid silver lining of Mexican hospitality
item.description: In their three weeks in Tijuana, Iran's players established a reciprocal relationship of love and respect with Mexicans.

item.title: Pakistan-administered Kashmir returns to daily life after deadly protests
item.description: Shops reopened and public transport services partially resumed in Pakistan-administered Kashmir after weeks of protests.

item.title: Nerves and tears as Germany exits World Cup on penalties
item.description: Germany fans watched from Berlin’s Sony Centre as Paraguay stunned the four-time world champion 4-3 on penalties.

item.title: Trump tells US petrol retailers to reduce prices ‘immediately’
item.description: US president warns of 'big problems' if retailers do not cut prices at the pumps, condemns illegal gouging.

item.title: Morocco beat Netherlands in dramatic World Cup shootout to reach last 16
item.description: Morocco come from behind to knock the Netherlands out in the round of 32, beating them 3-2 on penalties.

item.title: How Germany were handed a World Cup humbling and what it means to Paraguay
item.description: As well as Gill's heroics, Paraguay's unlikely victory was founded on coach Alfaro's unshakeable faith in his defenders.

item.title: US education faces ‘a crisis for low-wealth students’
item.description: Andre Perry tells This is America that going to college still pays, but low-wealth students carry most of the debt.

item.title: Police hunt for suspect after three wounded in Monaco blast
item.description: Ukrainian oligarch reported to be among injured in explosion at residential building in the Mediterranean principality.

item.title: Mourners light candles after deadly German shelter shooting
item.description: Residents lit candles on Monday evening near the site of a shooting that killed six staff members at a German shelter.

item.title: Trump: US envoys enroute to Doha for ‘perhaps important’ Iran meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump said US negotiators are heading to Qatar for what he called a ‘perhaps important’ meeting.

item.title: UN ordering 10,000 body bags for Venezuela as death toll expected to rise
item.description: The UN says it is procuring 10,000 body bags for Venezuela, warning the death toll will rise.

item.title: Fears for people deported from US to Venezuela hours before earthquakes hit
item.description: Search for deportees from US continues in the rubble of the hotel they were taken to in Venezuela's La Guaira city.
